title: Efficient Scientific Summarization
source_text:
Title: Efficient Scientific Summarization

Introduction:
Scientific papers are long and difficult to summarize faithfully. Existing methods often ignore important result sections.

Method:
We fine-tune a 3B parameter instruction model with lightweight adapters and section-aware prompts.

Results:
The proposed system improves ROUGE-L by 2.4 points over a prompt-only baseline and produces fewer unsupported claims.

Conclusion:
Adapter-based summarization improves quality while preserving efficiency.
target_summary: We present an adapter-based scientific summarization system that uses section-aware prompting to improve faithfulness and ROUGE-L over prompt-only baselines.

title: Hierarchical Long-Document Summarization
source_text:
Title: Hierarchical Long-Document Summarization

Introduction:
Long scientific documents exceed the capacity of simple single-pass summarizers.

Method:
We split papers by section, summarize each section, and synthesize a final abstract-style summary.

Results:
Hierarchical inference preserves result details better than truncation-based summarization on long papers.

Conclusion:
Section-aware hierarchical summarization is a practical approach for long-context scientific summarization.
target_summary: This work introduces section-aware hierarchical summarization for long scientific papers, improving preservation of result details compared with truncation-based methods.
